metadata.publisher: Food and Nutrition Board, Institute of Medicine, National Academies
notes: Se conservó el label original de etapa de vida en life_stage_raw.
También se generó life_stage normalizado cuando había guiones distintos o un aparente error de origen como '61-50 y'.
Los valores con asterisco (*) se marcaron con basis='AI' cuando correspondía.
ND se guardó como value=null y special='ND'.

estimated_average_requirements.title: Dietary Reference Intakes (DRIs): Estimated Average Requirements

footnotes.a: As retinol activity equivalents (RAEs). 1 RAE = 1 μg retinol, 12 μg β-carotene, 24 μg α-carotene, or 24 μg β-cryptoxanthin.
footnotes.c: As niacin equivalents (NE). 1 mg of niacin = 60 mg of tryptophan.
footnotes.d: As dietary folate equivalents (DFE). 1 DFE = 1 µg food folate = 0.6 µg of folic acid from fortified food or as a supplement consumed with food = 0.5 µg of a supplement taken on an empty stomach.

vitamins_rda_ai.title: Recommended Dietary Allowances and Adequate Intakes, Vitamins

footnotes.a: As retinol activity equivalents (RAEs). 1 RAE = 1 μg retinol, 12 μg β-carotene, 24 μg α-carotene, or 24 μg β-cryptoxanthin.
footnotes.b: As cholecalciferol. 1 µg cholecalciferol = 40 IU vitamin D.
footnotes.c: Under the assumption of minimal sunlight.
footnotes.e: As niacin equivalents (NE). 1 mg of niacin = 60 mg of tryptophan; 0-6 months = preformed niacin (not NE).
footnotes.g: Although AIs have been set for choline, there are few data to assess whether a dietary supply of choline is needed at all stages of the life cycle.
footnotes.h: Because 10 to 30 percent of older people may malabsorb food-bound B12, those older than 50 years should meet their RDA mainly with fortified foods or supplements.
footnotes.i: All women capable of becoming pregnant should consume 400 µg from supplements or fortified foods in addition to food folate from a varied diet.
footnotes.j: It is assumed that women will continue consuming 400 µg from supplements or fortified food until pregnancy is confirmed and prenatal care begins.

elements_rda_ai.title: Recommended Dietary Allowances and Adequate Intakes, Elements

total_water_and_macronutrients.title: Recommended Dietary Allowances and Adequate Intakes, Total Water and Macronutrients

footnotes.a: Total water includes all water contained in food, beverages, and drinking water.
footnotes.b: Based on g protein per kg of body weight for the reference body weight.

footnotes.a: Approximately 10 percent of the total can come from longer-chain n-3 or n-6 fatty acids.

rows.recommendation: As low as possible while consuming a nutritionally adequate diet
As low as possible while consuming a nutritionally adequate diet
As low as possible while consuming a nutritionally adequate diet
Limit to no more than 25 % of total energy

footnotes.a: Not a recommended intake. A daily intake of added sugars that individuals should aim for to achieve a healthful diet was not set.

footnotes.b: As α-tocopherol; applies to any form of supplemental α-tocopherol.
footnotes.c: The ULs for vitamin E, niacin, and folate apply to synthetic forms obtained from supplements, fortified foods, or a combination of the two.
footnotes.d: β-Carotene supplements are advised only to serve as a provitamin A source for individuals at risk of vitamin A deficiency.
footnotes.e: ND = Not determinable due to lack of data of adverse effects in this age group and concern with regard to lack of ability to handle excess amounts. Source of intake should be from food only.

footnotes.a: Although the UL was not determined for arsenic, there is no justification for adding arsenic to food or supplements.
footnotes.b: The ULs for magnesium represent intake from a pharmacological agent only and do not include intake from food and water.
footnotes.c: Although silicon has not been shown to cause adverse effects in humans, there is no justification for adding silicon to supplements.
footnotes.d: Although vanadium in food has not been shown to cause adverse effects in humans, there is no justification for adding vanadium to food and vanadium supplements should be used with caution.
footnotes.e: ND = Not determinable due to lack of data of adverse effects in this age group and concern with regard to lack of ability to handle excess amounts. Source of intake should be from food only.
